_rationale: Nested d10/d20 chosen over flat single-die mode to preserve real upset potential. The d10 controls ONLY how many d20s are rolled that turn and contributes no separate flat bonus or penalty of its own. A prior 'nestedPenalty' field applied a flat +/-10% based only on whether the d10 landed above or below its own mean, regardless of magnitude (a roll of 1 and a roll of 5 produced the identical adjustment) — this was removed since it added constant noise rather than real scaled uncertainty tied to the actual roll. 10 stages map loosely onto the internal shape of a single 3-week tick (one battle resolves within one tick), each stage representing roughly a few days of the standoff for tug-of-war visualization purposes.
_seedNote: Default seed for deterministic testing only. Production battles should pass a fresh seed per encounter (e.g., derived from world tick + combatant IDs) rather than reusing this default.
_calendarNote: 1 tick = 3 weeks. A battle resolves entirely within one tick regardless of internal stage count.

nestedDieMode_seedSweep.note: Sign flips freely across independent seeds (7 negative, 3 positive of 10), confirming variance is honest randomness rather than a directional bias. All individual batches passed tolerance.
